Gentlemen:
Louis J. Giza, 005-6575 a member of the Civilian
Conservation Corps, who was enrolled May 19th. 1933, at
Fort Knox, Ky. has been discharged the 1st. day of July,
1934; but previous to this discharge Louis J. Giza had
been injured by an accident, that WAS no fault of his
own, receiving what seemed to be a concussion of the brain.
This accident has made Louis J. Giza mentally deficient
and he has lost his memory.
Mr. Louis J. Giza Was the support of his parent,
Joseph Giza. Since the discharge of Louis J. Giza
from the Civilian Conservatory Corps due to the accident,
Mr. Joseph Giza, father of Louis J. Giza loses the
support and maintenance of his son.
It would please the father Joseph Giza, if the
Civilian Conservatory Corps of The United States would
make some appropriation for the disconfiture caused by
the loss of his son who was his sole support and
maintenance. Mr. Joseph Giza is sixty four years of age.
Begging to hear from you in the very near
future, I remain,

The Acts of February 15, 1934, and June 19, 1934, provide that
compensation shall be allowed for traumatic injuries sustained by
Civilian Conservation Corps enrollees only while in the performance
of duty, and the Acts further provide that traumatic injury shall
mean only injury by accident causing damage or harm to the physical
structure of the body and shall not include disease in any form except
as it shall naturally result from an injury.
It does not appear from the evidence submitted that you are entitled
to benefits of compensation for the following reason: no traumatic injury

OFFICIAL REPORT OF INJURY TO MEMBER OF CIVILIAN CONSERVATION CORPS
Under the act of September 7, 1916, as made applicable by acts
of February 15, 1934, and June 19, 1934
(A) INSTRUCTIONS
1. This form is for use only in reporting injuries to members of the Civilian Conservation Corps and takes the place of Forms
C. A. 1 and C. A. 2, referred to in the Regulations of the United Stater Employees' Compensation Commission. These regulations
show 1 be followed as far as applicable.
A report on this form will be forwarded to the Commission in every case in which a member of the Civilian Conservation
Cor (a) is disabled for work for more than 15 days by a traumatic injury by accident while in performance of duty in the
Corps; (b) may suffer any permanent disability as a result of such injury, irrespective of the duration of temporary disability;
(c) dise from any cause.
3. In case of disability the report will be forwarded on the thirtieth day after disability began or upon return to duty if
disability does not last 30 days, and in case of death as soon as practicable after death.
4. The injured person should complete the employee's statement (B), but if this is not practicable it may be completed
by a person designated to act in his behalf who must subscribe his own signature and permanent address in addition to that of
the injured person.
5. The immediate superior's statement should be completed by the work foreman or other person in charge of the injured
person at time of injury, who should state what he knows about the injury and when and how he acquired knowledge of it if
he was not an eyewitness, and whether injured member was engaged in supervised or authorized activities at time of injury.
6. Witnesses should state what they saw personally. If there was no actual eyewitness to the Injury, statements
should be obtained from persons first having knowledge of it, showing what they know about it and when and from whom the
information was obtained. Particular care should be exercised to obtain detailed statements from witnesses immediately
following the injury if it appears that injury was caused by negligence of a third party other than an employee or agency of
the United States.
7. The official superior's statement should be completed by the camp or company commander.

OFFICIAL REPORT OF INJURY TO MEMBER OF CIVILIAN CONSERVATION CORPS
Under the act of September 7, 1916, as made applicable by acts
of February 15, 1934, and June 19, 1934
(A) INSTRUCTIONS
1. This form is for use only in reporting injuries to members of the Civilian Conservation Corps and takes the place of Forms
C. A. I and C. A.2, referred to in the Regulations of the United States Employees' Compensation Commission. These regulations
should be followed as far as applicable.
2. A report on this form will be forwarded to the Commission in every case in which a member of the Civilian Conservation
Corps-(a) is disabled for work for more than 15 days by a traumatic injury by accident while in performance of duty in the
Corps; (b) may suffer any permanent disability as a result of such injury, irrespective of the duration of temporary disability;
(c) dies from any cause.
3. In case of disability the report will be forwarded on the thirtieth day after disability began or upon return to duty if
disability does not last 30 days, and in case of death as soon as practicable after death.
4. The injured person should complete the employee's statement (B), but if this is not practicable it may be completed
by a person designated to act in his behalf who must subscribe his own signature and permanent address in addition to that of
the injured person.
5. The immediate superior's statement should be completed by the work foreman or other person in charge of the injured
person at time of injury, who should state what he knows about the injury and when and how he acquired knowledge of it if
he was not an eyewitness, and whether injured member was engaged in supervised or authorized activities at time of injury.
6. Witnesses should state what they saw personally. If there was no actual eyewitness to the injury, statements
should be obtained from persons first having knowledge of it, showing what they know about it and when and from whom the
information was obtained. Particular care should be exercised to obtain detailed statements from witnesses immediately
following the injury if it appears that injury was caused by negligence of a third party other than an employee or agency of
the United States.
7. The official superior's statement should be completed by the eamp or company commander.
